records.title: From Within
records.summary: Youth Artist Residents exhibition reflecting on personal experiences, memories, and environments that shape each artist's creative voice.
records.description: Presented in the vitrine gallery, From Within highlights the work of Oolite Arts' Youth Artist Residents. Developed over the course of the Youth Artist Residency, the exhibition marks a period of experimentation, reflection, and growth through drawing, painting, sculpture, and mixed media. Featuring works by Ana Blanco, Noa Garcia, Emely Yanji, Melina Walsh, and TJ Wright.
records.featuredArtists: Ana Blanco; Noa Garcia; Emely Yanji; Melina Walsh; TJ Wright

records.title: Sites of the Self
records.summary: Studio Residents exhibition examining how identity is shaped, negotiated, and continually redefined.
records.description: Oolite Arts is pleased to present Sites of the Self, curated by René Morales, bringing together works by the current cohort of Oolite Studio Residents to examine the processes through which identity is shaped, negotiated, and continually redefined. Featuring works by Diego Gabaldon, Gonzalo Hernandez, Sepideh Kalani, Shayla Marshall, Bex McCharen, Lucía Morales, Genesis Moreno, Ana Mosquera, Sheherazade Thénard, Pangea Kali Virga, Nadia Wolff, Ricardo E. Zulueta, and José Delgado Zuñiga.
records.curator: René Morales, Senior Curatorial Fellow, Bakehouse Art Complex
records.featuredArtists: Diego Gabaldon; Gonzalo Hernandez; Sepideh Kalani; Shayla Marshall; Bex McCharen; Lucía Morales; Genesis Moreno; Ana Mosquera; Sheherazade Thénard; Pangea Kali Virga; Nadia Wolff; Ricardo E. Zulueta; José Delgado Zuñiga

records.title: Open Studios / Sites of the Self / From Within
records.summary: Open Studios evening with exhibitions by 2026 Studio and Youth Artist Residents, July 8, 6–9 p.m.
records.description: Presented in conjunction with Oolite Arts' Open Studios, visitors are invited to engage with exhibitions developed directly with Oolite Arts' 2026 Studio and Youth Artist Residents. Guests will experience works-in-progress and gain insight into the evolving nature of contemporary artistic practice.

records.title: The Fabric of Remembering: Cyanotype & Quilting
records.address: 924 Lincoln Rd., First Floor, Miami Beach, FL 33139
records.summary: A three-week hands-on workshop with Bex McCharen exploring cyanotype printing on fabric, quilting, storytelling, and memory.
records.description: Join artist Bex McCharen for this three-week workshop exploring fabric as a vessel for memory, storytelling, and legacy. Participants will cyanotype print family photographs onto fabric, then piece, sew, and embellish the printed images into small quilted works. All skill levels welcome.

records.title: Between a view and a milestone
records.summary: Group exhibition featuring 2018 Studio Residents, including Leo Castaneda, exploring place, topography, and history.
records.description: Between a view and a milestone provides a window into the distinct experiences and practices of Oolite Arts studio residents as they contemplate what defines neighborhoods, cities, and landscapes. Curated by Angelica Arbelaez.
records.featuredArtists: Leo Castaneda; Morel Doucet; Juan Pablo Garza; Adler Guerrier; Laura Marsh; Jillian Mayer; Terence Price II; Jamilah Sabur; Anastasia Samoylova; Alba Triana; Joshua Veasey

records.title: Resident Night: Oct. 2018
records.summary: Open studios and resident night with 2018–2019 studio residents including Leo Castaneda.

records.title: Resident Night: Jan. 2019
records.summary: Winter resident night with open studios for the 2018–2019 cohort.

records.title: Altered States of Consciousness: Opening Night + Resident Night
records.summary: Opening night and resident night for Altered States of Consciousness, curated by Phillip Karp.

records.title: Walgreens Window: Concepts — Demo(s): Levels & Bosses
records.summary: Leo Castaneda presented game-based work in Oolite Arts' Walgreens Window program.

records.title: Our New Home — Little River Campus
records.address: 75 NW 72nd Street area · Future Oolite Arts campus
records.summary: Oolite Arts' 26,850-square-foot capital project in Little River — a Barozzi Veiga-designed artist village with 24 studios, fabrication, classrooms, and public exhibition spaces.
records.description: A 26,850-square-foot campus on 1.3 acres in Little River, designed by Barozzi Veiga as a village of artists with LEED-targeted sustainability. Includes 24 artist studios, two exhibition spaces, fabrication space, classrooms for 400+ annual art classes, admin offices, and an accessible rooftop. Permitting underway; groundbreaking expected later this year. RFPs sent to five contractors with bids expected before end of July. Public amenities include exhibitions, courtyard, rooftop reception, classrooms, and fabrication. Design architect Barozzi Veiga (Barcelona); architect of record Charles Benson. Process informed by Rene Gonzalez, Reed Kroloff RFIs, Facilities Committee dialogue, value engineering, and community input including Clayton Campbell interviews.

records.title: 2027 Open Calls — The Ellies & Residencies
records.summary: Applications open for 2027 opportunities: The Ellies, Studio Residency, Home + Away, and Cinematic Residency. June 1 – July 31, 2026.
records.description: Apply through Submittable for 2027 The Ellies Creator Award, Art Teacher Travel Award, Curatorial Practice and Critical Writing Award, Studio Residency, Home + Away Residency, and Cinematic Residency. Overview at oolitearts.org/ellies-open-calls. Grant report and agreement forms available for current awardees.
